page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m4.2xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 8 dedicated physical cores on an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.5 GHz. The instance is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage and CPU-based processing. Network connectivity is limited to a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates average performance across standard compute and memory workloads. The instance achieved a single-core stress-ng score of 1828.39 and a multi-core score of 7717.41. Memory bandwidth is measured at 426.16 GB/sec for cached small blocks, dropping to 60.16 GB/sec for uncached large block reads. In application-specific benchmarks, the server demonstrated a static web serving throughput of 9.56 GB/sec and processed 7.81 million Redis SET operations per second.

While the instance handles lightweight machine learning tasks adequately, processing 732.07 tokens per second for a small 135M parameter LLM, it exhibits significant performance degradation on larger models. Prompt processing for a medium 7B model drops to 15.99 tokens per second, which is categorized in the bottom 10% of benchmarked hardware. Given the lack of local storage and moderate 2 Gbps network baseline, this instance is best suited for general-purpose workloads such as web serving, software compilation, database operations, and lightweight application hosting rather than data-intensive or large-scale AI inference.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m4.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 8 dedicated physical cores on an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor running at 2.5 GHz, paired with 32.0 GB of RAM. Operating on a KVM hypervisor, this server lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Benchmark results place the instance in the average tier for standard compute tasks, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 5300.0 and a static web serving throughput of 9.56 GB/sec. However, it exhibits clear performance limitations on larger workloads, such as a weak 7.29 tokens per second for 7B LLM text generation. Consequently, this instance is highly suited for balanced, non-storage-intensive workloads like web serving, software compilation, and moderate database operations, offering a stable resource profile without the overhead of specialized hardware.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m4.2xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose instance with 8 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 32 GB RAM, and a 2 Gbps network baseline, designed for balanced web serving and database workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m4.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 8 Intel Xeon cores, 32 GB RAM, and average compute performance for standard workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual server offering dedicated Intel Xeon processing and stable memory performance for standard web and database workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated Intel Xeon cores running at 2.5 GHz
32.0 GB of system memory with 4.0 GB per core
No local storage or GPU hardware included
Suited for web serving, software compilation, and database operations
Exhibits performance limitations on medium-sized LLM inference
